groups.title: Working at Milo's Restaurants
groups.desc:
Questions 1-5
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Benefits • $32$ provided for all staff • $33$ during weekdays at all Milo's Restaurants • $34$ provided after midnight Person specification • must be prepared to work well in a team • must care about maintaining a high standard of $35$ • must have a qualification in $36$

questions.title: · 【   】 provided for all staff
questions.analysis: 原文中明确指出"all employees get training"（所有员工都能得到培训）。这里的"training"（培训）正是题目中所问的"provided for all staff"（为所有员工提供）的内容。而且，原文还特别强调"even temporary staff"（甚至临时工），这进一步证实了培训确实是提供给所有员工的福利。此外，这句话是在讨论公司福利的上下文中提到的，与题目要求完全吻合。其他提到的福利，如制服或餐厅折扣，都不是为所有员工提供的，只有培训是明确指出适用于所有员工的。

questions.title: · 【   】 during weekdays at all Milo's Restaurants
questions.analysis: 它直接回答了题干中关于Milo's餐厅工作日福利的问题。原文中提到，作为在这家公司工作的一个好处，员工可以在他们的任何餐厅获得折扣。这个折扣适用于工作日，因为随后提到周末不适用（"Even at weekends?" "No, but you'll be working then anyway."）。因此，"discount"（折扣）是唯一符合题干要求的答案，它既适用于工作日，又适用于所有的Milo's餐厅。

questions.title: · 【   】 provided after midnight
questions.analysis: 题目询问的是午夜之后提供的服务。在原文中，我们可以清楚地听到，如果员工在午夜之后下班，公司会支付他们打车回家的费用。这里的"taxi"（出租车）就是公司提供的服务。这个信息直接回答了题目的要求，即午夜之后提供的服务。虽然随后男性提到他可能会骑自行车，但那是他个人的选择，而不是公司提供的服务。因此，正确答案是"taxi"。注意听力材料中的细节和上下文很重要，这可以帮助你区分公司提供的服务和个人选择。

questions.title: ·must care about maintaining a high standard of 【   】
questions.analysis: 题目要求填写一个词，表示"必须关心维持高标准的什么"。在原文中，说话者提到他们必须确保提供高水平的"service"（服务）。这直接对应了题目中的"high standard"（高标准）。"service"是餐饮业中的一个关键概念，表示对顾客的服务质量，这正是餐厅工作中需要维持高标准的重要方面。此外，"service"是唯一符合题目要求的单词答案，既满足了语境，又符合题目的语法要求。

questions.title: ·must have a qualification in 【   】
questions.analysis:
题目要求填写一个资格证书（qualification），而原文中明确提到了需要"have a certificate in English"（拥有英语证书）。这里的"certificate in English"就是所需的资格证明。

        此外，原文中提到这是"the only other thing that's required"（唯一其他需要的东西），强调了英语证书的重要性。同时，文中将英语证书与"good communication skills"（良好的沟通技能）联系在一起，进一步说明了为什么英语证书是必需的资格。

        题目中的"qualification"（资格）在原文中对应的是"certificate"（证书），这两个词在此上下文中可以视为同义词，都表示一种正式的能力认证。

        因此，"English"是符合题目要求的唯一正确答案，它既满足了"qualification"的要求，又符合原文中提到的具体证书类型。

groups.desc:
Questions 6-10
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

content:
Checking portions, etc. are correct
Making sure $38$ is clean

Starting salary £ $39$ per hour
Start work at 5.30 a.m.

Supporting senior chefs
Maintaining stock and organising $40$

Annual salary £23,000
No work on a $41$ once a month

questions.analysis:
：
        1. 题目要求填写的是地名，而不是完整的街道名。
        2. 对话中明确提到"The first one is in Wivenhoe Street"，其中"Wivenhoe"就是我们需要的地名。
        3. 说话人还特意拼写了这个地名："W-I-V-E-N-H-O-E"，这进一步强调了这个词的重要性。
        4. 虽然完整的地址是"Wivenhoe Street"，但题目只要求填写一个单词，因此正确答案就是"Wivenhoe"。
        5. 这个地名是一个专有名词，在听力材料中被明确指出和拼写，这是一个很强的信号，表明它很可能是答案。

题目要求填写与清洁相关的物品。在原文中，说话者提到了餐厅supervisor的职责，其中包括检查"cleaning the equipment"（清洁设备）的程序是否被遵循。这里的"equipment"（设备）就是我们需要填入的答案。它直接与清洁相关，且符合题目要求的名词形式。此外，"equipment"在餐厅环境中是一个常见词，指代需要清洁的各种器具和设备，完全符合题目上下文。虽然"procedures"（程序）这个词也出现在相关句子中，但它描述的是清洁的过程，而不是被清洁的物品，因此不是正确答案。正确理解这一点需要你对句子结构和上下文有准确的把握。
题目要求填写的是起始工资。在答案句中，"to begin with"（开始的时候）明确指出了9.75英镑是初始工资。虽然之后提到了三个月后工资会涨到11.25英镑，但我们需要填写的是刚开始工作时的工资金额。此外，题目要求填写的是一个词或数字，9.75正好符合这个要求。记住，在填写表格题时，我们通常需要填写最初提到的或最直接相关的信息。

题目要求填写junior chef的一项职责。在原文中，女士明确提到junior chef的职责包括"sorting out all the deliveries"（处理所有的送货）。这个职责直接对应了题目中需要填写的内容。虽然原文中还提到了其他职责，如支持副厨师长和管理库存，但"deliveries"是最符合题目要求的单词答案。此外，"deliveries"也与餐厅工作环境密切相关，符合整段对话的上下文。

原文中明确提到了员工的休息安排。虽然餐厅在周一休息（"they're closed on Mondays"），但这并不是员工的个人休息日。相反，文中明确指出每四周会有一个周日作为员工的休息日。这个信息直接回答了题目中关于员工休息日的问题。

        需要注意的是，这道题考察了对细节信息的准确把握。虽然文中提到了多个与时间和休息相关的信息，但只有"Sunday"是每四周一次的员工个人休息日。这要求考生能够仔细分辨不同信息之间的差异，并准确捕捉与题目要求相符的答案。

        此外，这个答案还体现了对工作时间安排的理解。在餐饮业中，周末通常是工作日，而员工的休息日可能会在工作日进行轮换。理解这一点有助于更好地理解听力材料中的信息。

transcript.en: WOMAN: So, I understand you're interested in restaurant work?
transcript.cn: 女士：好的，我了解到你有兴趣在餐厅工作，对吗？

transcript.en: MAN: Yes. I've got a bit of experience and I can provide references.
transcript.cn: 男士：是的。我有一些工作经验而且我可以提供推荐信。

transcript.en: WOMAN: That's good. I can check all that later.
transcript.cn: 女士：好的。后续我会再确认这些。

transcript.en: Now, Milo's Restaurants have some vacancies at the moment.
transcript.cn: 目前，米洛餐厅有一些岗位在招人。

transcript.en: They're a really good company to work for. Lots of benefits.
transcript.cn: 他们是一家非常好的公司，提供很多福利。

transcript.en: MAN: Oh right.
transcript.cn: 男士：明白了。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yes. They've got a very good reputation for looking after staff.
transcript.cn: 女士：嗯。他们在照顾员工方面是出了名的好。

transcript.en: For example, all employees get training - even temporary staff.
transcript.cn: 比如，所有的员工都有培训项目，就连临时工也可以参与培训。

transcript.en: MAN: Oh really? That's quite unusual, isn't it?
transcript.cn: 男士：是吗？确实不常见，不是吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN: Certainly is.
transcript.cn: 女士：就是说。

transcript.en: MAN: And do staff get free uniforms too?
transcript.cn: 男士：员工制服免费发吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN: Um … you just need to wear a white T-shirt and black trousers, it says here. So I guess not …
transcript.cn: 女士：这个嘛，这里写的是你需要穿白色T恤和黑色裤子。所以我猜，不是免费的。

transcript.en: But another benefit of working for a big company like this is that you can get a discount at any of their restaurants.
transcript.cn: 但是在大公司工作的另外一个好处是在公司旗下任一餐厅消费都可以打折。

transcript.en: MAN: Even at weekends?
transcript.cn: 男士：周末也是吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN: No, but you'll be working then anyway.
transcript.cn: 女士：不是的。反正你周末的时候也要上班。

transcript.en: MAN: Oh yes. I suppose so.
transcript.cn: 男士：是的，我想也是。

transcript.en: Most of their restaurants are in the city centre, aren't they?
transcript.cn: 他们大部分餐厅都在市中心对吗？

transcript.en: So, easy to get to by bus?
transcript.cn: 所以，坐公交好到吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yes. That's right.
transcript.cn: 女士：是的，没错。

transcript.en: But if you have to do a late shift and finish work after midnight, the company will pay for you to get a taxi home.
transcript.cn: 但是如果你需要上晚班，半夜才下班的话，公司会支付你打车回家的费用。

transcript.en: MAN: I probably won't need one.
transcript.cn: 男士：估计我用不上。

transcript.en: I think I'd use my bike.
transcript.cn: 我想我会骑车回家。

transcript.en: WOMAN: OK. Now, they do have some quite specific requirements for the kind of person they're looking for.
transcript.cn: 女士：没问题。好了，他们确实对他们要找的人有一些非常具体的要求。

transcript.en: Milo's is a young, dynamic company and they're really keen on creating a strong team.
transcript.cn: 米洛餐厅是一家年轻、充满活力的公司，他们非常热衷于打造一支强大的员工队伍。

transcript.en: It's really important that you can fit in and get on well with everyone.
transcript.cn: 能够融入团队并与每个人相处融洽真的非常重要。

transcript.en: MAN: Yeah. I've got no problem with that. It sounds good, actually.
transcript.cn: 男士：好的。我没有问题。实际上，这听起来很不错。

transcript.en: The last place I worked for was quite demanding too.
transcript.cn: 我上一个工作单位的要求也很高。

transcript.en: We had to make sure we gave a really high level of service.
transcript.cn: 我们需要确保我们提供高水平的服务。

transcript.en: WOMAN: That's good to hear because that will be equally important at Milo's.
transcript.cn: 女士：听你这么说我很高兴，因为在米洛餐厅这也同样重要。

transcript.en: I know they want people who have an eye for detail.
transcript.cn: 我知道他们想要注重细节的人。

transcript.en: MAN: That's fine. I'm very used to working in that kind of environment.
transcript.cn: 男士：没问题。我已经非常习惯在这样的环境中工作。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Perfect. So the only other thing that's required is good communication skills, so you'll need to have a certificate in English.
transcript.cn: 女士：太好了。除此之外，公司还有一个要求就是员工要有良好的沟通能力，所以你需要有英语的证书。

transcript.en: MAN: Sure.
transcript.cn: 男士：好的。

transcript.en: WOMAN: OK. Let's have a look at the current job vacancies at Milo's.
transcript.cn: 女士：嗯。让我们看下米洛餐厅目前在招人的岗位。

transcript.en: The first one is in Wivenhoe Street.
transcript.cn: 第一家在威文霍街。

transcript.en: MAN: Sorry, where?
transcript.cn: 男士：不好意思，哪里？

transcript.en: WOMAN: Wivenhoe. W-I-V-E-N-H-O-E. It's quite central, just off Cork Street.
transcript.cn: 女士：威文霍。W-I-V-E-N-H-O-E。

transcript.en: MAN: Oh right.
transcript.cn: 男士：好的。

transcript.en: WOMAN: They're looking for a breakfast supervisor.
transcript.cn: 女士：招聘的是早餐主管。

transcript.en: MAN: That would be OK.
transcript.cn: 男士：那没问题。

transcript.en: WOMAN: So you're probably familiar with the kind of responsibilities involved.
transcript.cn: 女士：所以你可能熟悉岗位涉及的工作责任。

transcript.en: Obviously checking that all the portions are correct, etc., and then things like checking all the procedures for cleaning the equipment are being followed.
transcript.cn: 很明显，你需要确认所有的餐品菜量合适。还要确认设备的清理步骤完整。

transcript.en: MAN: OK. And what about the salary?
transcript.cn: 男士：明白了。薪水怎么样？

transcript.en: In my last job I was getting £9.50 per hour.
transcript.cn: 上一份工作的时薪是9.5镑。

transcript.en: I was hoping to get a bit more than that.
transcript.cn: 我希望这次能多一些。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Well, to begin with, you'd be getting £9.75 but that goes up to £11.25 after three months.
transcript.cn: 女士：嗯，一开始的薪酬是9.75镑，但是三个月之后会提高到11.25镑。

transcript.en: MAN: That's not too bad.
transcript.cn: 男士：还不错。

transcript.en: And I suppose it's a very early start?
transcript.cn: 我猜这份工作需要一大早开始？

transcript.en: WOMAN: Mmm. That's the only unattractive thing about this job.
transcript.cn: 女士：嗯。这也是这个岗位唯一不吸引人的地方。

transcript.en: But then you have the afternoons and evenings free.
transcript.cn: 但是工作之后下午和晚上都是自由的。

transcript.en: So the restaurant starts serving breakfast from 7 a.m.
transcript.cn: 餐厅早上七点开始供应早餐。

transcript.en: And you'd have to be there at 5.30 to set everything up.
transcript.cn: 你需要5点半到岗，开始准备好一切。

transcript.en: But you'd be finished at 12.30.
transcript.cn: 下班时间是12点半。

transcript.en: MAN: Mmm. Well, as you say, there are advantages to that.
transcript.cn: 男士：好吧。正如您所说，早上班也有好处。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Now, you might also be interested in the job at the City Road branch.
transcript.cn: 女士：你也许也对城市路分店的工作有兴趣。

transcript.en: That's for a junior chef, so again a position of responsibility.
transcript.cn: 那里在招聘初级厨师，也是一份职责明确的工作。

transcript.en: MAN: I might prefer that, actually.
transcript.cn: 男士：实际上我更喜欢这份工作。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Right, well obviously this role would involve supporting the sous chef and other senior staff.
transcript.cn: 女士：明白。很明显，这份工作涉及到辅助副主厨和其它的资深员工。

transcript.en: And you'd be responsible for making sure there's enough stock each week - and sorting out all the deliveries.
transcript.cn: 而且你要负责确保餐厅的每周库存充足，还要整理送来的货物。

transcript.en: MAN: I've never done that before, but I imagine it's fairly straightforward, once you get the hang of it.
transcript.cn: 男士：我之前从没做过这样的工作。但是我想一旦掌握了工作流程，应该还挺容易的。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yes, and you'd be working alongside more experienced staff to begin with, so I'm sure it wouldn't be a problem.
transcript.cn: 女士：是的，而且一开始你会和更有经验的员工一起工作，所以我相信这不会有问题。

transcript.en: The salary's slightly higher here.
transcript.cn: 这份工作的薪水更高。

transcript.en: It's an annual salary of £23,000.
transcript.cn: 年薪在23000镑。

transcript.en: MAN: Right.
transcript.cn: 男士：好的。

transcript.en: WOMAN: I know that if they like you, it's likely you'll be promoted quite quickly.
transcript.cn: 女士：我还知道如果他们欣赏你，你很快就可以得到晋升。

transcript.en: So that's worth thinking about.
transcript.cn: 所以这个机会很值得认真考虑。

transcript.en: MAN: Yes. It does sound interesting.
transcript.cn: 男士：是的，听起来确实很好。

transcript.en: What are the hours like?
transcript.cn: 工作时间怎么样？

transcript.en: WOMAN: The usual, I think.
transcript.cn: 女士：我觉得就是常规时间。

transcript.en: There's a lot of evening and weekend work, but they're closed on Mondays.
transcript.cn: 晚上和周末的工作多，但是餐厅周一不营业。

transcript.en: But you do get one Sunday off every four weeks.
transcript.cn: 但是每四周可以休一个星期天。

transcript.en: So would you like me to send off your …
transcript.cn: 所以你需要我给你发送一下……

groups.title: What are the TWO main reasons why this site has been chosen for the housing development?
groups.desc:
Questions 11 and 12
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: A
list.content: It has suitable geographical features.

list.title: B
list.content: There is easy access to local facilities.

list.title: C
list.content: It has good connections with the airport.

list.title: D
list.content: The land is of little agricultural value.

list.title: E
list.content: It will be convenient for workers.

collect_option.title: What are the TWO main reasons why this site has been chosen for the housing development?

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B和E。选项B正确是因为原文提到了医院和学校这些当地设施的便利性，这符合"easy access to local facilities"的描述。选项E正确是因为原文明确指出由于新的工业中心，对员工住房有很大需求，这与"convenient for workers"相对应。这两个原因被作为主要考虑因素提出，因此是选择该地点进行住房开发的两个主要原因。
这道题的正确答案是B和E。选项B正确是因为原文提到了医院和学校这些当地设施的便利性，这符合"easy access to local facilities"的描述。选项E正确是因为原文明确指出由于新的工业中心，对员工住房有很大需求，这与"convenient for workers"相对应。这两个原因被作为主要考虑因素提出，因此是选择该地点进行住房开发的两个主要原因。

groups.title: Which TWO aspects of the planned housing development have people given positive feedback about?
groups.desc:
Questions 13 and 14
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: A
list.content: the facilities for cyclists

list.title: B
list.content: the impact on the environment

list.title: C
list.content: the encouragement of good relations between residents

list.title: D
list.content: the low cost of all the accommodation

list.title: E
list.content: the rural location

collect_option.title: Which TWO aspects of the planned housing development have people given positive feedback about?

questions.analysis: 这道题要求选择两个人们对计划中的住房开发项目给予积极反馈的方面。根据原文，正确答案是B和C。选项B反映了人们对项目环境影响的积极反馈，原文提到项目设计很好地融入了自然景观，并保护了树木和野生动物。选项C体现了人们对项目促进居民良好关系的积极反馈，原文提到该开发项目的设计和设施使各个年龄段的人可以幸福地生活在一起。这两个选项直接对应了题干中要求的积极反馈内容。
这道题要求选择两个人们对计划中的住房开发项目给予积极反馈的方面。根据原文，正确答案是B和C。选项B反映了人们对项目环境影响的积极反馈，原文提到项目设计很好地融入了自然景观，并保护了树木和野生动物。选项C体现了人们对项目促进居民良好关系的积极反馈，原文提到该开发项目的设计和设施使各个年龄段的人可以幸福地生活在一起。这两个选项直接对应了题干中要求的积极反馈内容。

groups.desc:
Questions 15-20
Label the map below.
Write the correct letter, A-I, next to Questions 15-20.

questions.title: School
questions.analysis: 根据听力原文，“community centre”位于开发区的“northeast side”，并且紧邻那些专为65岁以上居民设计的房屋。地图中G位置处于开发区的东北方向，且旁边标有“Housing for the elderly”，符合描述。因此，正确答案是G。

questions.title: Sports centre
questions.analysis: 根据听力原文，学校的位置是在南入口进入后第二个左转的拐角处。而地图上的C点正是这个位置。通过确定定位词“School”，并在听力原文中寻找对应的描述“the school will be on that road, at the corner of the second turning to the left”，可以准确定位到学校的位置是C点。因此，第16题的正确答案是C。

questions.title: Clinic
questions.analysis: 第17题要求找出地图上诊所的位置。听力材料中明确提到“诊所会在湖的下方，右边有一条街的房屋”。湖在地图上标记为“Lake”，而诊所在其下方，符合这个描述的位置是标记为“D”的区域。因此，正确答案是“D”。学生在做题时应注意“clinic”和“lake”作为定位词，以及“below”作为信号词。

questions.title: Community centre
questions.analysis: 答案为B，因为听力原文明确提到"northeast side"（东北侧）和"residents over 65"（65岁以上的居民），对应题目中的"housing for the elderly"。在地图中，B标记的位置位于东北方向，且符合描述。

questions.title: Supermarket
questions.analysis: 题目要求在地图上标注超市的位置。根据原文描述，超市位于开发区域的两个入口之间，并且靠近伦敦路的三棵大树南边。结合地图信息，可以确定超市的位置在H点附近。因此，第19题的正确答案是H。

questions.title: Playground
questions.analysis: 第20题的正确答案是A。根据听力原文，游乐场位于从南入口出发的道路的左侧弯道上，并且可以看到湖景。地图上唯一符合这个描述的位置是A。学生在听力过程中应特别注意“playground”、“South Entrance”、“curve”、“lake”等关键词，并在地图上找到对应的位置。

transcript.en: Hello everyone.
transcript.cn: 大家好。

transcript.en: It's good to see that so many members of the public have shown up for our presentation on the new housing development planned on the outskirts of Nunston.
transcript.cn: 很高兴看到有这么多人出席我们关于在宁斯顿郊区规划的新住宅开发区的介绍活动。

transcript.en: I'm Mark Reynolds and I'm Communications Manager at the development.
transcript.cn: 我是开发部的传讯经理马克·雷诺兹。

transcript.en: I'll start by giving you a brief overview of our plans for the development.
transcript.cn: 首先，我将简要介绍一下我们的开发计划。

transcript.en: So one thing I'm sure you'll want to know is why we've selected this particular site for a housing development.
transcript.cn: 好了。我相信您肯定想知道我们为什么特别选择此处进行住房开发。

transcript.en: At present it's being used for farming, like much of the land around Nunston.
transcript.cn: 目前它被用于耕种，就像宁斯顿周围的大部分土地一样。

transcript.en: But because of the new industrial centre in Nunston, there's a lot of demand for housing for employees in the region, as many employees are having to commute long distances at present.
transcript.cn: 但因为宁斯顿的新工业中心，员工对住房的需求很大，目前许多员工不得不选择长途通勤。

transcript.en: Of course, there's also the fact that we have an international airport just 20 minutes' drive away, but although that's certainly convenient, it wasn't one of our major criteria for choosing the site.
transcript.cn: 当然，还有一个事实是这里距离国际机场仅20分钟车程，这确实很方便，但这并不是我们选择这里的主要标准之一。

transcript.en: We were more interested in the fact that there's an excellent hospital just 15 kilometres away, and a large secondary school even closer than that.
transcript.cn: 我们更感兴趣的是距离此处仅15公里的地方就有一家一流的医院，而且更近的地方还有一所大的中学。

transcript.en: One drawback to the site is that it's on quite a steep slope, but we've taken account of that in our planning so it shouldn't be a major problem.
transcript.cn: 此处的一个缺点是它位于相当陡峭的斜坡上。但我们在规划中已考虑到这一点，因此这应该不是一个主要问题。

transcript.en: We've had a lot of positive feedback about the plans.
transcript.cn: 我们收到了很多关于规划计划的积极反馈。

transcript.en: People like the wide variety of accommodation types and prices, and the fact that it's only a short drive to get out into the countryside from the development.
transcript.cn: 人们喜欢规划中显示的多样住宿类型和不同的价格，也喜欢从开发区到乡村只需很短的车程。

transcript.en: We were particularly pleased that so many people liked the designs for the layout of the development, with the majority of people saying it generally made a good impression and blended in well with the natural features of the landscape, with provision made for protecting trees and wildlife on the site.
transcript.cn: 我们特别高兴的是，有这么多人喜欢开发区的布局设计，大多数人表示该设计总体上给人留下了良好的印象，并与景观的自然特征很好地融合。此外规划还为保护此处的树木和野生动物给出了应对措施。

transcript.en: Some people have mentioned that they'd like to see more facilities for cyclists, and we'll look at that, but the overall feedback has been that the design and facilities of the development make it seem a place where people of all ages can live together happily.
transcript.cn: 有些人提到他们希望看到更多供骑自行车者使用的设施，我们会考虑这一点。但总体反馈是，该开发区的设计和设施使其看起来像是一个适合所有年龄段的人快乐地居住的地方。

transcript.en: OK. So I'll put a map of the proposed development up on the screen.
transcript.cn: 好了。所以我会在屏幕上放一张拟议开发的地图。

transcript.en: You'll see it's bounded on the south side by the main road, which then goes on to Nunston.
transcript.cn: 大家可以看到开发区的南边是通向宁斯顿的主干道。

transcript.en: Another boundary is formed by London Road, on the western side of the development.
transcript.cn: 开发区的西侧边界是伦敦路。

transcript.en: Inside the development there'll be about 400 houses and 3 apartment blocks.
transcript.cn: 在开发区中，将有大约400栋房屋和3栋公寓楼。

transcript.en: There'll also be a school for children up to 11 years old.
transcript.cn: 还将有一所供11岁以下儿童就读的学校。

transcript.en: If you look at the South Entrance at the bottom of the map, there's a road from there that goes right up through the development.
transcript.cn: 如果大家看一下地图底部的南门，就会看到那里有一条路一直向上穿过开发区。

transcript.en: The school will be on that road, at the corner of the second turning to the left.
transcript.cn: 学校就在那条路上，在第二个左转弯的拐角处。

transcript.en: A large sports centre is planned with facilities for indoor and outdoor activities.
transcript.cn: 配备了室内和室外活动设施的大型运动中心也在规划之中。

transcript.en: This will be on the western side of the development, just below the road that branches off from London Road.
transcript.cn: 它位于开发区的西侧，就在伦敦路的支路下方。

transcript.en: There'll be a clinic where residents can go if they have any health problems.
transcript.cn: 这里还会有一个诊所。如果居民有任何健康问题，他们可以去那里就医。

transcript.en: Can you see the lake towards the top of the map?
transcript.cn: 大家能看到地图顶部的湖吗？

transcript.en: The clinic will be just below this, to the right of a street of houses.
transcript.cn: 诊所就在这下面，在一排房屋的右边。

transcript.en: There'll also be a community centre for people of all ages.
transcript.cn: 这里还会有一个为所有年龄段的人建立的社区中心。

transcript.en: On the northeast side of the development, there'll be a row of specially designed houses specifically for residents over 65, and the community centre will be adjoining this.
transcript.cn: 在开发区的东北侧，会有一排专为65岁以上居民设计的房屋，社区中心将与之相邻。

transcript.en: We haven't forgotten about shopping.
transcript.cn: 我们没有忘记购物。

transcript.en: There'll be a supermarket between the two entrances to the development.
transcript.cn: 开发区的两个入口之间将有一家超市。

transcript.en: We're planning to leave the three large trees near London Road, and it'll be just to the south of these.
transcript.cn: 我们计划保留在伦敦路附近的三棵大树，超市就在这些树的南边。

transcript.en: It's planned to have a playground for younger children.
transcript.cn: 为年幼的孩子建造的游乐场也在规划中。

transcript.en: If you look at the road that goes up from the South Entrance, you'll see it curves round to the left at the top, and the playground will be in that curve, with nice views of the lake.
transcript.cn: 如果你看一下从南入口向上的道路，你会发现道路向左拐向地图上方，操场就在那个弯曲处，可以看到美丽的湖景。

transcript.en: OK, so now does anyone …
transcript.cn: 好的，现在有人……

groups.desc:
Questions 21-24
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: Why do the students think the Laki eruption of 1783 is so important?

options.title: A
options.content: It was the most severe eruption in modern times.

options.title: B
options.content: It led to the formal study of volcanoes.

options.title: C
options.content: It had a profound effect on society.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C，因为原文明确指出Laki火山喷发对政治事件、社会和经济都产生了重大影响。这种广泛的影响正是题目中所问的"profound effect on society"（对社会的深远影响）。原文中使用了"significant"、"impacted"、"consequences"等词语，强调了这次事件对社会各方面的深远影响，这与选项C最为吻合。

questions.title: What surprised Adam about observations made at the time?

options.title: A
options.content: the number of places producing them

options.title: C
options.content: the lack of scientific data to support them

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A选项。Adam表示他对当时已经建立了如此多的气象站感到惊讶。这直接对应了选项A中提到的"观测地点的数量"。在原文中，Adam说："I was surprised there were so many weather stations established by that time"，这句话明确表达了他对观测点数量之多的惊讶。"so many"这个表达直接对应了选项A中的"the number of"，表明Adam对观测地点的数量感到惊讶。此外，"weather stations"与题干中的"observations"相对应，因为气象站就是用来进行天气观测的。

questions.title: According to Michelle, what did the contemporary sources say about the Laki haze?

options.title: A
options.content: People thought it was similar to ordinary fog.

options.title: B
options.content: It was associated with health issues.

options.title: C
options.content: It completely blocked out the sun for weeks.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是B，因为原文明确提到当时的报告将头痛、呼吸问题和哮喘发作的增加归咎于Laki雾霾。这些症状都是健康问题，与选项B "It was associated with health issues" 直接对应。原文中提供了具体的健康问题示例，这些都可以归类为"health issues"。

questions.title: Adam corrects Michelle when she claims that Benjamin Franklin

options.title: A
options.content: came to the wrong conclusion about the cause of the haze.

options.title: B
options.content: was the first to identify the reason for the haze.

options.title: C
options.content: supported the opinions of other observers about the haze.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B，因为Adam纠正了Michelle的说法，指出虽然Benjamin Franklin经常被认为是第一个识别出雾霾原因的人，但实际上一位法国自然学家比他更早发现了这一点。这表明Franklin并不是第一个识别出雾霾原因的人，从而否定了选项B的说法。

groups.title: Which TWO issues following the Laki eruption surprised the students?
groups.desc:
Questions 25 and 26
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: A
list.content: how widespread the effects were

list.title: B
list.content: how long-lasting the effects were

list.title: C
list.content: the number of deaths it caused

list.title: D
list.content: the speed at which the volcanic ash cloud spread

list.title: E
list.content: how people ignored the warning signs

collect_option.title: Which TWO issues following the Laki eruption surprised the students?

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A和B。学生们对Laki火山爆发的两个影响感到惊讶：影响范围之广（A）和影响时间之长（B）。原文中提到，即使在火山爆发两年后，仍然在北美和北非等遥远地区观察到异常天气现象，这体现了影响范围之广。同时，学生们也对影响持续时间之长感到惊讶，这从"the effects would go on for so long"这句话中可以看出。这两点都超出了学生们的预期，因此成为让他们感到惊讶的主要原因。

这道题的正确答案是A和B。学生们对Laki火山爆发的两个影响感到惊讶：影响范围之广（A）和影响时间之长（B）。原文中提到，即使在火山爆发两年后，仍然在北美和北非等遥远地区观察到异常天气现象，这体现了影响范围之广。同时，学生们也对影响持续时间之长感到惊讶，这从"the effects would go on for so long"这句话中可以看出。这两点都超出了学生们的预期，因此成为让他们感到惊讶的主要原因。

groups.title: Comments
groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
What comment do the students make about the impact of the Laki eruption on the following countries?
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to Questions 27-30.

list.title: A
list.content: This country suffered the most severe loss of life.

list.title: B
list.content: The impact on agriculture was predictable.

list.title: C
list.content: There was a significant increase in deaths of young people.

list.title: D
list.content: Animals suffered from a sickness.

list.title: E
list.content: This country saw the highest rise in food prices in the world.

list.title: F
list.content: It caused a particularly harsh winter.

questions.title: Iceland
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D选项"Animals suffered from a sickness"。在听力材料中，描述了冰岛牲畜因食用被火山灰污染的植被而中毒的情况。这种中毒可以被理解为一种疾病或病症，因此与选项D最为吻合。材料中使用的"poisoned"（中毒）直接表明了动物遭受了健康问题，符合"suffered from a sickness"的描述。

questions.title: Egypt
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A选项，因为原文中明确提到埃及因饥荒导致的死亡人数比任何其他国家都多。这直接对应了选项A中"This country suffered the most severe loss of life"（这个国家遭受了最严重的生命损失）的描述。原文还强调这种影响比瘟疫还要严重，进一步突出了埃及所遭受的生命损失程度。

questions.title: UK
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C。听力材料中提到，在英国，死亡率显著上升，约为平常的两倍。更重要的是，材料特别指出有异常高比例的25岁以下人群死亡。这直接对应了选项C中提到的"年轻人死亡人数显著增加"。这个信息准确地反映了Laki火山爆发对英国的影响，特别是对年轻人群的影响。

questions.title: USA
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是F（It caused a particularly harsh winter），因为听力材料中明确描述了美国遭遇了异常严重的冬季天气。原文提到George Washington在日记中记录了他们在弗吉尼亚州被大雪困住直到3月份，而密西西比河上还出现了前所未有的浮冰。这些描述清楚地表明美国经历了一个特别寒冷的冬天。"被大雪困住直到3月"和"密西西比河上漂浮着冰块"都是严冬的明显特征，而"前所未有的"这个词进一步强调了这种冬季天气的异常性。

transcript.en: ADAM: So, Michelle, shall we make a start on our presentation?
transcript.cn: 亚当：那么，米歇尔，我们可以开始准备演讲了吗？

transcript.en: We haven't got that much time left.
transcript.cn: 我们没有那么多时间了。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: No, Adam. But at least we've done all the background reading.
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：是的，亚当。但至少我们已经完成了所有背景阅读。

transcript.en: I found it really interesting - I'd never even heard of the Laki eruption before this.
transcript.cn: 我发现这真的很有趣。在此之前我从未听说过拉基火山喷发。

transcript.en: ADAM: Me neither.
transcript.cn: 亚当：我也没有。

transcript.en: I suppose 1783 is a long time ago.
transcript.cn: 我想1783年是很久以前了。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: But it was a huge eruption and it had such devastating consequences.
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：但那是一次巨大的喷发，造成了毁灭性的后果。

transcript.en: ADAM: I know.
transcript.cn: 亚当：我知道。

transcript.en: It was great there were so many primary sources to look at.
transcript.cn: 有这么多原始资源可供查看很方便。

transcript.en: It really gives you a sense of how catastrophic the volcano was.
transcript.cn: 它真的让你感受到火山的灾难程度。

transcript.en: People were really trying to make sense of the science for the first time.
transcript.cn: 人们第一次真正地试图理解科学。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: That's right.
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：没错。

transcript.en: But what I found more significant was how it impacted directly and indirectly on political events, as well as having massive social and economic consequences.
transcript.cn: 但我发现更重要的是它如何直接和间接地影响政治事件，以及产生巨大的社会和经济后果。

transcript.en: ADAM: I know. That should be the main focus of our presentation.
transcript.cn: 亚当：我知道。这应该是我们演讲的重点。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: The observations made by people at the time were interesting, weren't they?
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：当时人们的观察很有趣，不是吗？

transcript.en: I mean, they all gave a pretty consistent account of what happened, even if they didn't always use the same terminology.
transcript.cn: 我的意思是，他们都对发生的事情给出了非常一致的描述，即使他们使用的术语不尽相同。

transcript.en: ADAM: Yeah. I was surprised there were so many weather stations established by that time - so, you know, you can see how the weather changed, often by the hour.
transcript.cn: 亚当：没错。我很惊讶那个时候建立了这么多气象站。所以，你知道，你能看到天气是如何变化的，通常是一小时一个样。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: Right. Writers at the time talked about the Laki haze to describe the volcanic fog that spread across Europe.
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：对。当时的作家们用拉基雾霾来形容席卷欧洲的火山雾。

transcript.en: They all realised that this wasn't the sort of fog they were used to - and of course this was in pre-industrial times - so they hadn't experienced sulphur-smelling fog before.
transcript.cn: 他们都意识到这不是他们习惯的那种雾，当然那是在前工业时代，所以他们以前没有感受过硫磺味的雾。

transcript.en: ADAM: No, that's true.
transcript.cn: 亚当：是的，的确没见过。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: Reports from the period blamed the haze for an increase in headaches, respiratory issues and asthma attacks.
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：该时期的报告将头痛、呼吸系统问题和哮喘发作的增加归咎于雾霾。

transcript.en: And they all describe how it covered the sun and made it look a strange red colour.
transcript.cn: 他们都描述了它如何遮住太阳，使它看起来是一种奇怪的红色。

transcript.en: ADAM: Must have been very weird.
transcript.cn: 亚当：当时一定很奇怪。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: It's interesting that Benjamin Franklin wrote about the haze.
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：有趣的是，本杰明·富兰克林写了关于雾霾的文章。

transcript.en: Did you read that?
transcript.cn: 你读过吗？

transcript.en: He was the American ambassador in Paris at the time.
transcript.cn: 他当时是美国驻巴黎大使。

transcript.en: ADAM: Yeah. At first no one realised that the haze was caused by the volcanic eruption in Iceland.
transcript.cn: 亚当：是的。起初没有人意识到雾霾是由冰岛火山喷发造成的。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: It was Benjamin Franklin who realised that before anyone else.
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：本杰明·富兰克林比任何人都先意识到这一点。

transcript.en: ADAM: He's often credited with that, apparently.
transcript.cn: 亚当：显然，这经常被认为是他的功劳。

transcript.en: But a French naturalist beat him to it - I can't remember his name.
transcript.cn: 但一位法国博物学家抢在他之前发现了这一事实。我不记得他的名字了。

transcript.en: I'd have to look it up.
transcript.cn: 我得查一下。

transcript.en: Then other naturalists had the same idea - all independently of each other.
transcript.cn: 然后其他博物学家也有同样的想法。他们的观点是相互独立的。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: Oh right. We should talk about the immediate impact of the eruption, which was obviously enormous - especially in Iceland, where so many people died.
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：哦，对了。我们应该谈谈火山喷发的直接影响，这显然是巨大的，尤其是在冰岛，那里有那么多人丧生。

transcript.en: ADAM: Mmm. You'd expect that - and the fact that the volcanic ash drifted so swiftly - but not that the effects would go on for so long.
transcript.cn: 亚当：嗯。人们能预测到火山灰会迅速飘散，但没有预计到的是这种影响会持续这么久。

transcript.en: Or that two years after the eruption, strange weather events were being reported as far away as North America and North Africa.
transcript.cn: 或者可以谈一下在火山喷发两年后，远至北美和北非都出现了奇怪的天气事件。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: No. I found all that hard to believe too.
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：是的。我也觉得难以置信。

transcript.en: It must have been terrible - and there was nothing anyone could do about it, even if they knew the ash cloud was coming in their direction.
transcript.cn: 这一定很可怕。而且即使他们知道火山灰云正朝他们的方向袭来，任何人都无能为力。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: We should run through some of the terrible consequences of the eruption experienced in different countries.
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：我们应该回顾一下不同国家经历的火山喷发的一些可怕后果。

transcript.en: There's quite a varied range.
transcript.cn: 范围很广。

transcript.en: ADAM: Starting with Iceland, where the impact on farming was devastating.
transcript.cn: 亚当：首先是冰岛，那里对农业的影响是毁灭性的。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: Mmm. One of the most dramatic things there was the effect on livestock as they grazed in the fields.
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：嗯。最引人注目的事情之一是对在田间吃草的牲畜的影响。

transcript.en: They were poisoned because they ate vegetation that had been contaminated with fluorine as a result of the volcanic fallout.
transcript.cn: 它们中毒是因为它们食用了因火山沉降物而被氟污染的植物。

transcript.en: ADAM: That was horrible.
transcript.cn: 亚当：那太可怕了。

transcript.en: In Egypt, the bizarre weather patterns led to a severe drought and as a result the Nile didn't flood, which meant the crops all failed.
transcript.cn: 在埃及，怪异的天气模式导致了严重的干旱，结果尼罗河没有泛滥，这意味着农作物全部欠收。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: It's so far from where the eruption happened and yet the famine there led to more people dying than any other country.
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：它离火山爆发的地方很远，但那里的饥荒导致的死亡人数比其他任何国家都多。

transcript.en: It was worse than the plague.
transcript.cn: 这比瘟疫还糟糕。

transcript.en: ADAM: OK. Then in the UK the mortality rate went up a lot - presumably from respiratory illnesses.
transcript.cn: 亚当：好的。然后在英国，死亡率上升了很多，大概是由于呼吸道疾病。

transcript.en: According to one report it was about double the usual number and included an unusually high percentage of people under the age of 25.
transcript.cn: 根据一份报告，这一数字大约是平时因该疾病死亡人数的两倍，其中包括异常高比例的25岁以下人群。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: Mmm. I think people will be surprised to hear that the weather in the USA was badly affected too.
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：嗯。我想人们听到美国的天气也受到严重影响时会感到惊讶。

transcript.en: George Washington even makes a note in his diary that they were snowbound until March in Virginia.
transcript.cn: 乔治·华盛顿甚至在他的日记中记下他们在弗吉尼亚州一直被大雪困到3月。

transcript.en: That was before he became president.
transcript.cn: 那是在他成为总统之前。

transcript.en: ADAM: Yes, and there was ice floating down the Mississippi, which was unprecedented.
transcript.cn: 亚当：是的，密西西比河上漂浮着冰，这是前所未有的。

transcript.en: MICHELLE: Astonishing, really. Anyway, what do you think …
transcript.cn: 米歇尔：真的很惊人。不管怎样，你怎么看……

groups.title: Pockets
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Reason for choice of subject • They are $58$ but can be overlooked by consumers and designers. Pockets in men's clothes • Men started to wear $59$ in the 18th century. • A $60$ sewed pockets into the lining of the garments. • The wearer could use the pockets for small items. • Bigger pockets might be made for men who belonged to a certain type of $61$ . Pockets in women's clothes • Women's pockets were less $62$ than men's. • Women were very concerned about pickpockets. • Pockets were produced in pairs using $63$ to link them together. • Pockets hung from the women's $64$ under skirts and petticoats. • Items such as $65$ could be reached through a gap in the material. • Pockets, of various sizes, stayed inside clothing for many decades. • When dresses changed shape, hidden pockets had a negative effect on the $66$ of women. • Bags called 'pouches' became popular, before women carried a $67$

questions.title: ·They are 【   】 but can be overlooked by consumers and designers.
questions.analysis: 题目要求填写口袋的一个特性，而原文中明确指出口袋是非常方便（convenient）的。原文强调了即使我们携带包或公文包，口袋仍然是最方便的存放小物品的地方。这与题干中提到的"overlooked"（被忽视的）形成对比，说明尽管口袋非常方便，但人们往往不太关注它们。这种对比关系突出了口袋的"convenient"（方便）特性，因此"convenient"是正确答案。

questions.title: ·Men started to wear 【   】 in the 18th century.
questions.analysis: 题目询问的是18世纪男性开始穿什么，而原文中明确指出了在讨论18世纪男性时尚时，西装（suits）成为了流行的服饰。"became popular"这个表达清楚地表明西装在这个时期开始被广泛穿着。此外，文章在这之后详细描述了西装的各个部分（如裤子、背心和夹克），进一步证实了"suits"是正确答案。这个答案直接回答了题目中关于18世纪男性开始穿着什么的问题。

questions.title: · A 【   】 sewed pockets into the lining of the garments.
questions.analysis: 原文中明确提到了"pockets were sewn into this cloth by whichever tailor the customer used"（口袋是由顾客选用的裁缝缝在这层布料上的）。这里的"tailor"（裁缝）就是我们需要填写的答案。这个句子使用了被动语态，表明执行缝制动作的是"tailor"。此外，"tailor"这个词恰好符合题目要求的"ONE WORD ONLY"（仅一个单词）。这个答案直接回答了题干中"谁缝制了口袋"的问题，而且与原文信息完全吻合。

questions.title: ·Bigger pockets might be made for men who belonged to a certain type of 【   】 .
questions.analysis: 原文中明确提到了某些职业（profession）的人需要更大的口袋。文中使用"profession"这个词来概括需要携带特殊工具（如医疗器械）的各种职业。虽然文中举例提到了医生（doctor）和内科医生（physician），但题目要求的是更广泛的类别，而不是具体的职业。"profession"正好符合这个要求，它包含了各种需要专业技能和知识的职业。此外，文中提到这些职业的人需要更大（larger）但更朴素（plainer）的口袋，这正好呼应了题干中的"bigger pockets"。因此，"profession"是最合适的答案，它既符合原文的描述，又满足题目要求的概括性。

questions.title: ·Women's pockets were less 【   】 than men's.
questions.analysis: 原文中直接比较了女性口袋和男性口袋的特征。题目要求填写一个词来描述女性口袋相比男性口袋的特点，而原文中提到"they weren't nearly as visible or as easy to reach as men's"（它们不像男性的口袋那样容易看到或容易够到）。在这个比较中，"visible"（可见的）是最直接对应题目要求的词。此外，题目要求只填一个词，而"visible"比"easy to reach"更直接地对应了题干中的比较。因此，"visible"是最合适的答案。

questions.title: ·Pockets were produced in pairs using 【   】 to link them together.
questions.analysis:
题目要求填写用来连接成对口袋的物品。在原文中，我们可以清楚地听到"a pair of pockets made that were tied together with string"这句话。这里的"string"（绳子）正是用来连接这对口袋的物品。

        需要注意的是，虽然原文中提到口袋是用布料（fabric）制作的，但题目询问的是连接口袋的物品，而不是制作口袋的材料。因此，正确答案是"string"而不是"fabric"或其他材料。

        此外，"string"这个词在日常生活中很常见，指的是细绳或线，恰好符合连接口袋的功能。这个答案既符合题目要求，又与原文内容完全吻合。

questions.title: ·Pockets hung from the women's 【   】 under skirts and petticoats.
questions.analysis: 原文中明确提到了女性将口袋系在腰部（waist）。这个答案直接对应了题干中所问的位置，即口袋悬挂的位置。原文使用"tied around their waist"（系在腰部）来描述口袋的位置，这与题干中的"hung from"（悬挂）相呼应。此外，原文中的"beneath their clothes"（衣服下面）也与题干中的"under skirts and petticoats"（在裙子和衬裙下）相对应。这些细节都清楚地指向"waist"（腰部）作为正确答案。

questions.title: ·Items such as 【   】 could be reached through a gap in the material.
questions.analysis: 题目询问的是通过材料中的缝隙可以拿到什么物品。在原文中，作者描述了18世纪和19世纪女性的衣着习惯，特别提到了她们会在裙子的褶皱中留有开口，以便能够拿取携带的物品。文中明确举例说明可以通过这个开口拿取的物品是香水（perfume）。这个例子完全符合题目所问的情况，即通过材料中的缝隙（在这里指的是裙子褶皱中的开口）可以拿到的物品。此外，香水作为一种个人随身物品，也符合当时女性携带贵重或私人物品的习惯。因此，"perfume"是最准确和最符合上下文的答案。

questions.title: ·When dresses changed shape, hidden pockets had a negative effect on the 【   】 of women.
questions.analysis: 原文中明确提到了"detracted from the woman's image"（影响了女性的形象）。当题目问到隐藏的口袋对女性产生了什么负面影响时，"image"（形象）是最准确的答案。原文描述了当时裙子变得更紧身，使得原本隐藏的口袋变得明显，这直接影响了女性的整体形象。"Image"这个词在原文中被直接使用，而且完美地符合题目要求的"negative effect"（负面影响）。此外，"image"作为一个单词，也符合题目要求的"ONE WORD ONLY"（仅填一个单词）。所以，"image"是最准确和最符合题目要求的答案。

questions.title: ·Bags called 'pouches' became popular, before women carried a 【   】
questions.analysis: 原文中清楚地描述了女性携带物品方式的演变过程。首先出现的是"pouches"（小袋子），然后文中明确指出这些"pouches"发展成了更现代的"handbag"（手提包）。题目要求填写在"pouches"之后出现的物品，因此"handbag"是正确答案。此外，文中使用了"led on to"这个短语，明确表示了从"pouches"到"handbag"的演变顺序，进一步确认了答案的正确性。

transcript.en: Good morning.
transcript.cn: 早上好。

transcript.en: Now, we've been asked to choose an aspect of European clothing or fashion and to talk about its development over time.
transcript.cn: 现在，有人要求我们选择欧洲服装或时尚的一个方面，并探讨其随时间而产生的变化。

transcript.en: I decided to focus on a rather small area of clothing and that's pockets.
transcript.cn: 我决定聚焦在服装上很小的一部分，那就是口袋。

transcript.en: I chose pockets for two reasons, really.
transcript.cn: 我选择口袋有两个原因。

transcript.en: We all have them - in jeans, jackets, coats, for example - and even though we often carry bags or briefcases as well, nothing is quite as convenient as being able to pop your phone or credit card into your pocket.
transcript.cn: 我们的衣服，比如牛仔裤、夹克、外套，都有口袋。尽管我们也经常背背包或公文包，但没有什么比将手机或信用卡放入口袋更方便的了。

transcript.en: Yet, I suspect that, other than that, people don't really think about pockets too much and they're rather overlooked as a fashion item.
transcript.cn: 然而，我怀疑，除了这个作用，人们不会过多地思考口袋的意义。而且，作为一种时尚单品，口袋往往被忽视了。

transcript.en: It's certainly very interesting to go back in time and see how pockets developed for men and women.
transcript.cn: 有趣的是我们可以回到过去，看看男性和女性的口袋是如何发展变化的。

transcript.en: In the 18th century, fashions were quite different from the way they are now, and pockets were too.
transcript.cn: 世纪的时尚与现在大不相同，口袋的样式也天差地别。

transcript.en: If we think about male fashion first … that was the time when suits became popular.
transcript.cn: 如果我们先聊男性时尚，会发现那是西装流行的时期。

transcript.en: Trousers were knee-length only and referred to as 'breeches', the waistcoats were short and the jackets were long, but all three garments were lined with material and pockets were sewn into this cloth by whichever tailor the customer used.
transcript.cn: 西裤被称为“马裤”，裤长只到膝盖。背心短，夹克长。但这三件都有里衬。不论哪一个裁缝都会把口袋封在内衬上。

transcript.en: The wearer could then carry small objects such as pencils or coins on their person and reach them through a gap in the lining.
transcript.cn: 然后，穿西装的人可以随身携带铅笔或硬币等小物件，通过衬里的缝隙就能够到它们。

transcript.en: Coat pockets became increasingly decorative on the outside for men who wanted to look stylish, but they were often larger but plainer if the wearer was someone with a profession who needed to carry medical instruments - a doctor or physician, for example.
transcript.cn: 对于想要看起来时尚的男士来说，外套的外侧口袋变得越来越具有装饰性。但如果穿衣服的人从事的是需要携带医疗器械的职业——例如医生或医师，他们的口袋通常会更大但更朴素。

transcript.en: The development of women's pockets was a little different.
transcript.cn: 女士口袋的发展略有不同。

transcript.en: For one thing, they weren't nearly as visible or as easy to reach as men's.
transcript.cn: 一方面，女装口袋不像男装口袋那样显眼，也不容易够到。

transcript.en: In the 18th and 19th centuries, women carried numerous possessions on their person and some of these could be worth a lot of money.
transcript.cn: 在18和19世纪，女性随身携带着许多财物，其中一些可能价值不菲。

transcript.en: Women were more vulnerable to theft and wealthy women, in particular, worried constantly about pickpockets.
transcript.cn: 女性更容易被盗窃，尤其是富有的女性，她们总是担心扒手。

transcript.en: So - what they did was to have a pair of pockets made that were tied together with string.
transcript.cn: 所以她们用的是用绳子系在一起的口袋。

transcript.en: The pockets were made of fabric, which might be recycled cloth if the wearer had little money or something more expensive, such as linen, sometimes featuring very delicate embroidery.
transcript.cn: 口袋是用布料做的。如果她们没有钱或更贵的材料，例如亚麻布，也可以使用旧布。有时口袋上带有非常精致的刺绣。

transcript.en: Women tied the pockets around their waist so that they hung beneath their clothes.
transcript.cn: 女人把口袋系在腰上，这样口袋就挂在衣服下面。

transcript.en: Remember, skirts were long then and there was plenty of room to hide a whole range of small possessions between the layers of petticoats that were commonly worn.
transcript.cn: 请记住，那时的裙子很长。在通常穿着的衬裙层之间有足够的空间来隐藏各种小物件。

transcript.en: They would have an opening in the folds of their skirts through which they could reach whatever they needed, like their perfume.
transcript.cn: 她们的裙子褶皱处会有一个开口，她们可以通过这个开口拿到任何需要的东西，比如香水。

transcript.en: Working women, of course, also needed to carry around items that they might use for whatever job or trade they were involved in, but their pairs of pockets still remained on the inside of their clothing, they just got bigger or longer - sometimes reaching down to their knees!
transcript.cn: 当然，职业女性也需要随身携带她们可能用于办公或者做买卖所需的任何物品，但她们的口袋仍然留在衣服内侧，只是变大或变长，有时会到膝盖处！

transcript.en: So the tie-on pockets went well into the 19th century and only changed when fashion altered towards the end of that period.
transcript.cn: 因此，系带口袋一直延续到19世纪，直到世纪末时尚发生变化时才有了改变。

transcript.en: That's when dresses became tighter and less bulky, and the pairs of pockets became very noticeable - they stood out too much and detracted from the woman's image.
transcript.cn: 那时裙子变得更紧身，不再笨重。一对袋子变得非常引人注目，太过突出，有损女性形象。

transcript.en: Women who had been used to carrying around a range of personal possessions - and still wanted to - needed somewhere to carry these items about their person.
transcript.cn: 习惯于随身携带各种个人物品的女性不愿意改变，所以需要找个地方装下随身携带的这些物品。

transcript.en: That was when small bags, or pouches as they were known, came into fashion and, of course, they inevitably led on to the handbag of more modern times, particularly when fashion removed pockets altogether.
transcript.cn: 那时小包也就是众所周知的手袋开始流行，当然，当时尚风潮彻底抛弃口袋的时候，手袋不可避免地演变成了更具现代感的手提包。
